verses.text: King Arad attacks; Israel vows and destroys the Canaanites at Hormah. Hormah. God gives victory to the vowing people.
King Arad attacks; Israel vows and destroys the Canaanites at Hormah. Hormah. God gives victory to the vowing people.
King Arad attacks; Israel vows and destroys the Canaanites at Hormah. Hormah. God gives victory to the vowing people.
The people are discouraged and speak against God and Moses. Murmuring. They loathe the manna again.
The people are discouraged and speak against God and Moses. Murmuring. They loathe the manna again.
Fiery serpents bite them; they confess and beg Moses to pray. Bitten. Sin brings a sting.
Fiery serpents bite them; they confess and beg Moses to pray. Bitten. Sin brings a sting.
Moses makes a brazen serpent; whoever looks lives. The serpent. A look of faith heals — a type of Christ lifted up.
Moses makes a brazen serpent; whoever looks lives. The serpent. A look of faith heals — a type of Christ lifted up.
They journey on, by Oboth and to the brooks of Arnon. The way. They press toward the land.
They journey on, by Oboth and to the brooks of Arnon. The way. They press toward the land.
They journey on, by Oboth and to the brooks of Arnon. The way. They press toward the land.
They journey on, by Oboth and to the brooks of Arnon. The way. They press toward the land.
A book is quoted of the bounds of Arnon. Recorded. Their march is marked.
A book is quoted of the bounds of Arnon. Recorded. Their march is marked.
At Beer they sing, 'Spring up, O well.' The well. God gives water and a song.
At Beer they sing, 'Spring up, O well.' The well. God gives water and a song.
At Beer they sing, 'Spring up, O well.' The well. God gives water and a song.
They journey to Pisgah, looking toward Jeshimon. Onward. The promise draws nearer.
They journey to Pisgah, looking toward Jeshimon. Onward. The promise draws nearer.
Sihon refuses passage and is defeated; Israel takes his land. Sihon. The first foe east of Jordan falls.
Sihon refuses passage and is defeated; Israel takes his land. Sihon. The first foe east of Jordan falls.
Sihon refuses passage and is defeated; Israel takes his land. Sihon. The first foe east of Jordan falls.
Sihon refuses passage and is defeated; Israel takes his land. Sihon. The first foe east of Jordan falls.
Sihon refuses passage and is defeated; Israel takes his land. Sihon. The first foe east of Jordan falls.
His cities are taken, with a song mocking Heshbon's fall. Heshbon. The conquest is sung.
His cities are taken, with a song mocking Heshbon's fall. Heshbon. The conquest is sung.
His cities are taken, with a song mocking Heshbon's fall. Heshbon. The conquest is sung.
His cities are taken, with a song mocking Heshbon's fall. Heshbon. The conquest is sung.
His cities are taken, with a song mocking Heshbon's fall. Heshbon. The conquest is sung.
Israel dwells in Sihon's land and takes Jaazer. Settled. They begin to possess.
Israel dwells in Sihon's land and takes Jaazer. Settled. They begin to possess.
Og of Bashan comes out; God says, 'Fear him not'; he is slain. Og. The second king falls by God's hand.
Og of Bashan comes out; God says, 'Fear him not'; he is slain. Og. The second king falls by God's hand.
Og of Bashan comes out; God says, 'Fear him not'; he is slain. Og. The second king falls by God's hand.
